title: kalmia ericoides var. aggregata
intro: Kalmia ericoides var. aggregata, also known as the low-growing shrub mountain laurel, is a plant species that belongs to the Ericaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Kalmia ericoides var. aggregata include its potential to help with wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
